articles.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
articles.summary: Meta launches Muse Spark, its first AI model after restructuring efforts, powering Meta AI apps and integrating across WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, and smart glasses.

articles.title: The vibes are off at OpenAI
articles.summary: OpenAI faces internal instability despite raising $122 billion in funding and maintaining ChatGPT's market dominance. Recent executive reshuffles and discontinued projects raise concerns about the company's long-term stability.

articles.title: Unionized ProPublica staff are on strike over AI, layoffs, and wages
articles.summary: ProPublica's unionized staff strikes for 24 hours over unresolved contract issues including AI protections, job security, layoff safeguards, and wages after two years of negotiations.

articles.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT
articles.summary: Tubi launched the first streaming app integration within ChatGPT, allowing users to access content directly through the AI chatbot.

articles.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
articles.summary: Astropad's Workbench enables remote monitoring and control of AI agents on Mac Minis via iPhone or iPad with low-latency streaming, reimagining remote desktop for AI rather than traditional IT support.

articles.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation
articles.summary: OpenAI released a Child Safety Blueprint to combat the rising child sexual exploitation linked to AI advancements. The initiative addresses safety concerns in artificial intelligence development.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The startup aims to address limitations of legacy platforms for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200 monthly for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers equivalent features for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscriptions.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI-encoded tokens and a coding challenge that attracted thousands of engineers and successfully hired talent.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence agency GRU hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agent tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access to perform tasks. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions to autonomous AI tools.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Three new Rowhammer attacks on Nvidia GPUs enable malicious users to gain complete root control of shared cloud machines by exploiting memory bit flip vulnerabilities in DRAM hardware.

articles.title: Mustafa Suleyman: AI development won’t hit a wall anytime soon—here’s why
articles.summary: Mustafa Suleyman argues AI development won't plateau soon due to exponential growth trends, not linear ones. Training data for frontier AI models has grown 1 trillion times since 2010, defying human intuition.

articles.title: The Download: water threats in Iran and AI’s impact on what entrepreneurs make
articles.summary: Desalination plants in Iran face vulnerability amid escalating conflict, with threats to destroy infrastructure that supplies critical water resources to the Middle East region.

articles.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
articles.summary: Middle East desalination plants face escalating vulnerability amid regional conflict, with Iran accusing the US of attacking a facility supplying water to 30 villages in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve continuously during job execution rather than relying solely on pre-training. This on-the-job learning approach enhances agent performance and adaptability in real-world tasks.

articles.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
articles.summary: Safetensors, a secure file format for storing machine learning models, is joining the PyTorch Foundation. This integration aims to enhance model safety and standardization across the PyTorch ecosystem.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on devices. The new model enhances on-device intelligence capabilities.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations shaping the next phase of artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available for developers and farmers to improve farming practices through AI.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to help developers balance cost and latency in their applications.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling users to generate high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map aimed at protecting Brazil's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model improves precision and reduces latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions with enhanced reliability.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI-driven manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI development.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics to 4K photorealism in real-time by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions to generate enhanced details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI news including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: AI’s software development success and central management needs
articles.summary: AI has entered early production for many enterprises, but adoption outpaces governance and integration capabilities. Companies must establish controls and integrate AI into existing platforms to manage risks effectively.

articles.title: Microsoft open-source toolkit secures AI agents at runtime
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for runtime security of AI agents, addressing concerns about autonomous language models executing code and accessing corporate networks faster than traditional policy controls can manage.

articles.title: Asylon and Thrive Logic bring physical AI to enterprise perimeter security
articles.summary: Asylon and Thrive Logic partner to deploy physical AI robots for enterprise perimeter security, combining autonomous patrols with AI analytics for real-time threat detection and response.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging capabilities for collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges of localization, self-localization, and path planning in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are becoming the competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that infrastructure, not just models, defines AI moats.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware targets local AI agents running on Mac Minis by exploiting macOS binaries to steal credentials as developers adopt OpenClaw AI agents.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical AI model limitations for real-world applications.

articles.title: Z.AI Introduces GLM-5.1: An Open-Weight 754B Agentic Model That Achieves SOTA on SWE-Bench Pro and Sustains 8-Hour Autonomous Execution
articles.summary: Z.AI released GLM-5.1, a 754B open-weight agentic model achieving state-of-the-art on SWE-Bench Pro with strong coding capabilities and 8-hour autonomous execution. Built with DSA and MoE architecture for efficient long-context processing.

articles.title: How to Combine Google Search, Google Maps, and Custom Functions in a Single Gemini API Call With Context Circulation, Parallel Tool IDs, and Multi-Step Agentic Chains
articles.summary: Tutorial on combining Google Search, Maps, and custom functions in Gemini API calls using context circulation and parallel tool IDs for multi-step agentic chains. Demonstrates five progressive demos enabling real-time location data integration.

articles.title: How to Deploy Open WebUI with Secure OpenAI API Integration, Public Tunneling, and Browser-Based Chat Access
articles.summary: Tutorial on deploying Open WebUI in Google Colab with secure OpenAI API integration, environment configuration, and public tunnel access for browser-based chat functionality.

articles.title: This AirTag alternative with a 130dB siren and strobe light has my name written all over it
articles.summary: The Pebblebee Halo is a Bluetooth tracker alternative featuring a 130dB siren and strobe light, functioning as a personal safety device beyond standard tracking capabilities.

articles.title: How to quickly convert EPUB files to Kindle format - and why it matters
articles.summary: Users can convert EPUB files to Kindle format, gaining more control over their digital library. This process breaks free from Amazon's format limitations.

articles.title: Your Kindle's not obsolete, it just needs a jailbreak - and I'll show you how it's done
articles.summary: Old Kindle devices can be jailbroken to become open-source readers, even if bricked. This guide shows how to transform and repurpose outdated e-readers.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: A practical guide for testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. Learn evaluation techniques for assessing agent performance and reliability.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: The article discusses race conditions in multi-agent systems where concurrent writes to shared resources produce unpredictable results. It highlights the practical challenges of coordinating multiple agents accessing the same data simultaneously.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: The article discusses five reranking models that enhance retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems by improving result quality and relevance in information retrieval tasks.

articles.title: Why AI Is Training on Its Own Garbage (and How to Fix It)
articles.summary: AI models are increasingly training on their own generated content, creating quality degradation issues. The article explores this problem and proposes solutions to maintain data integrity.

articles.title: Detecting Translation Hallucinations with Attention Misalignment
articles.summary: Researchers present a low-cost method for detecting hallucinations in neural machine translation through attention misalignment analysis, enabling token-level uncertainty estimation without expensive computational overhead.

articles.title: How to Use Claude Code to Build a Minimum Viable Product
articles.summary: Learn how to effectively present product ideas by building MVPs with Claude Code, a coding agent that streamlines the development process for minimum viable products.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights NVIDIA's physical AI breakthroughs and robots transforming industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local, on-device AI execution across RTX GPUs, data centers, and personal AI supercomputers, enabling efficient agentic AI without cloud dependency.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a new 3D real-time strategy game.

articles.title: Customize Amazon Nova models with Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now enables fine-tuning of Amazon Nova models for custom business needs. The platform supports supervised fine-tuning and other customization approaches to embed proprietary knowledge and workflows.

articles.title: Human-in-the-loop constructs for agentic workflows in healthcare and life sciences
articles.summary: AI agents in healthcare require human-in-the-loop constructs to ensure regulatory compliance and patient safety. AWS services enable practical implementation of human oversight at critical decision points.

articles.title: Building intelligent audio search with Amazon Nova Embeddings: A deep dive into semantic audio understanding
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enable intelligent audio search by converting audio content into searchable numerical vectors, capturing acoustic features like tone, emotion, and environmental sounds beyond traditional text-based methods.

articles.title: Integrate Physical AI Capabilities into Existing Apps with NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries
articles.summary: NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries enable developers to integrate Physical AI capabilities into existing applications, allowing teams to design and validate robots in simulated environments before production.

articles.title: Running AI Workloads on Rack-Scale Supercomputers: From Hardware to Topology-Aware Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA's GB200 and GB300 NVL72 supercomputers use Blackwell architecture with 18 compute trays and high-bandwidth networking for AI workloads, requiring topology-aware scheduling optimization.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 codec accelerates vision AI pipelines by improving throughput in decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages to match advancing model performance.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030 through developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology by bringing fresh perspectives and unique approaches to the field.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understanding of model behavior. Feature attribution analysis helps isolate input features driving predictions, advancing safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder to map objects to images and quantifies measurement quality through noise models and information estimation.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm replaces temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
